skills.description: This skill provides guidance and best practices for implementing secure API design patterns including authentication, authorization, input validation, rate limiting, and protection against common vulnerabilities.

when working with cross-platform security analysis
when the user asks about verifying skill legitimacy or performing security reviews

modifying the code being audited

skills.description: Identify and exploit authentication and session management vulnerabilities in web applications.
anti_triggers: Performing unauthorized testing

skills.description: A comprehensive codebase audit before pushing to GitHub, covering cleaning up junk files, fixing .gitignore, auditing source files, security checks, scalability, architecture, performance, documentation, and testing.
trigger_conditions: User requests 'audit the codebase' or 'review before push'
Before making the first push to GitHub
anti_triggers: After initial development and before final review
When pushing minor changes without critical issues

skills.description: Analyze project dependencies for known vulnerabilities, licensing issues, outdated packages, and provide actionable remediation strategies.
trigger_conditions: Checking license compliance or supply-chain risks
Identifying outdated packages and upgrade paths
anti_triggers: The project has no dependency manifests
You cannot change or update dependencies
The task is unrelated to dependency management

skills.description: Identify and exploit HTML injection vulnerabilities by testing web applications for reflected and stored HTML injections.
trigger_conditions: When assessing the security of a web application for potential HTML injection vulnerabilities.
During penetration testing to identify weak points in web applications.
anti_triggers: When dealing with user input that does not involve rendering HTML content.
In scenarios where JavaScript execution is required
as HTML injection alone does not execute scripts.

skills.description: Provide systematic methodologies for identifying and exploiting Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) vulnerabilities in web applications.
trigger_conditions: When testing a web application for potential IDOR vulnerabilities
When multiple user accounts are available for verification
anti_triggers: When the target application uses session-bound tokens instead of IDs
When rate limiting blocks enumeration attempts

skills.description: Autonomously deep-scan entire codebase line-by-line, understand architecture and patterns, then systematically transform it to production-grade, corporate-level professional quality with optimizations.
anti_triggers: user wants to manually review code
user prefers manual fixes

trigger_conditions: Performing security audits on web applications
anti_triggers: Using this workflow for non-security-related tasks
For general development or maintenance activities

skills.description: Execute comprehensive SQL injection vulnerability assessments on web applications to identify database security flaws, demonstrate exploitation techniques, and validate input sanitization mechanisms.
trigger_conditions: When a web application is suspected of having SQL injection vulnerabilities
During regular security audits or penetration testing engagements
anti_triggers: When the target application has proper input validation and sanitization mechanisms in place
In environments where unauthorized access to production databases is not allowed
